title: COMMAND GETKEYS
description: Extracts the key names from an arbitrary command.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of arguments to the command

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns [Array reply]() of keys from a full Redis command. `COMMAND GETKEYS` is a helper command to let you find the keys from a full Redis command. [`COMMAND`]() provides information on how to find the key names of each command (see `firstkey`, [key specifications](), and `movablekeys`), but in some cases it's not possible to find keys of certain commands and then the entire command must be parsed to discover some / all key names. You can use `COMMAND GETKEYS` or [`COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS`]() to discover key names directly from how Redis parses the commands. ## Examples COMMAND GETKEYS MSET a b c d e f COMMAND GETKEYS EVAL "not consulted" 3 key1 key2 key3 arg1 arg2 arg3 argN COMMAND GETKEYS SORT mylist ALPHA STORE outlist ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): list of keys from the given command. **RESP3:** [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of keys from the given command.
intro:
Returns [Array reply]() of keys from a full Redis command.

`COMMAND GETKEYS` is a helper command to let you find the keys
from a full Redis command.

[`COMMAND`]() provides information on how to find the key names of each command (see `firstkey`, [key specifications](), and `movablekeys`),
but in some cases it's not possible to find keys of certain commands and then the entire command must be parsed to discover some / all key names.
You can use `COMMAND GETKEYS` or [`COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS`]() to discover key names directly from how Redis parses the commands.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns [Array reply]() of keys from a full Redis command.

`COMMAND GETKEYS` is a helper command to let you find the keys
from a full Redis command.

[`COMMAND`]() provides information on how to find the key names of each command (see `firstkey`, [key specifications](), and `movablekeys`),
but in some cases it's not possible to find keys of certain commands and then the entire command must be parsed to discover some / all key names.
You can use `COMMAND GETKEYS` or [`COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS`]() to discover key names directly from how Redis parses the commands.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
COMMAND GETKEYS MSET a b c d e f
COMMAND GETKEYS EVAL "not consulted" 3 key1 key2 key3 arg1 arg2 arg3 argN
COMMAND GETKEYS SORT mylist ALPHA STORE outlist

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): list of keys from the given command.

**RESP3:**

[Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of keys from the given command.
